_comment: Per-service config. THE single file to update when AI services change. Re-verify quarterly. UI strings + paths verified live via claude-in-chrome on _last_verified date.

chatgpt.name: ChatGPT
chatgpt.where_to_paste_human: On the web: open ChatGPT → Settings (sidebar) → Personalization → turn on "Enable customization" → paste into the Custom Instructions field. On phone: Settings → Customize ChatGPT.
chatgpt.notes: Custom instructions apply to all chats. OpenAI's help article (updated April 2026) confirms 1500 character limit on the longer-form text fields. The two-box pattern ("What would you like ChatGPT to know about you?" / "How would you like ChatGPT to respond?") is what we generate for, but the help article phrasing is ambiguous about whether the UI now consolidates these — verify before each release.

claude.name: Claude
claude.where_to_paste_human: Open Claude → Projects (left sidebar) → "+ New project" → give it a name → paste the whole template into the description field (placeholder reads "Describe your project, goals, subject, etc…").
claude.notes: Projects keep your instructions separate from your normal chats. Requires Claude Pro for full features. Note: Claude also has a "Customize" page (claude.ai/customize) but that's for Skills + Connectors + Plugins (enterprise/power-user features), not user-level personalization — Projects is the right destination for a chatprep template.

gemini.name: Gemini
gemini.where_to_paste_human: Open Gemini → "Gems" in the left sidebar → "+ New Gem" → fill in the name and short description, then paste the whole template into the large "Enter a prompt for Gemini" instructions field.
gemini.notes: Gemini has a stricter length limit than the others — we automatically trim optional sections to fit. Char limit (~4000) is widely reported but Google does not publish an exact number; verify via the in-UI counter before each release.
